plan.goal: Investigate and reduce slow PRISM refresh-path latency by measuring the true hot path, isolating the dominant costs in no-op and small-delta refreshes, deciding whether one or more relevant optimizations from docs/OPTIMIZATIONS.md should be adopted during the tuning work, and validating the resulting latency improvements without regressing correctness. If the agent decides to include one or more such optimization items, this plan must be extended to track that added scope explicitly.
plan.title: Investigate and reduce slow PRISM refresh-path latency by measuring the true hot path, isolating the dominant costs in no-op and small-delta refreshes, deciding whether one or more relevant optimizations from docs/OPTIMIZATIONS.md should be adopted during the tuning work, and validating the resulting latency improvements without regressing correctness. If the agent decides to include one or more such optimization items, this plan must be extended to track that added scope explicitly.

graph.title: Investigate and reduce slow PRISM refresh-path latency by measuring the true hot path, isolating the dominant costs in no-op and small-delta refreshes, deciding whether one or more relevant optimizations from docs/OPTIMIZATIONS.md should be adopted during the tuning work, and validating the resulting latency improvements without regressing correctness. graph.goal: Investigate and reduce slow PRISM refresh-path latency by measuring the true hot path, isolating the dominant costs in no-op and small-delta refreshes, deciding whether one or more relevant optimizations from docs/OPTIMIZATIONS.md should be adopted during the tuning work, and validating the resulting latency improvements without regressing correctness. If the agent decides to include one or more such optimization items, this plan must be extended to track that added scope explicitly.

nodes.title: Instrument and benchmark the refresh path for no-op, one-file, and contention cases

acceptance.label: Covers at least no-op read refresh, one-file-write refresh, and busy-lock or deferred-refresh behavior
Produces a concrete phase-by-phase latency breakdown for the steady-state refresh hot path

nodes.title: Identify the dominant refresh costs across fs refresh, materialization checks, runtime sync, and persisted-state interactions

acceptance.label: Names the concrete dominant costs instead of only aggregate query latency
Separates true compute cost from lock waiting, reload checks, and derived cache or persistence work

nodes.title: Evaluate relevant OPTIMIZATIONS.md items and decide whether any should be folded into refresh tuning

acceptance.label: If one or more optimization items are adopted, this plan is extended before implementation to track the added work explicitly
Records which optimization items from docs/OPTIMIZATIONS.md are in or out of scope for this effort and why

nodes.title: Implement the selected refresh-path fixes and any approved optimization work

acceptance.label: Keeps correctness and scope behavior intact while reducing latency
Targets the measured bottlenecks on the common read-side refresh path

nodes.title: Validate latency improvements and regression risk for request-path and background refresh behavior

acceptance.label: Confirms no correctness regressions in request-path freshness, deferred refresh, or background runtime sync behavior
Demonstrates improved latency for the common refresh scenarios under test
